The VTRC bus company serves cities in the USA. A subset $S$ of the cities is
called well-served if it has at least three cities and from every city $A$ in
$S$, one can take a nonstop VTRC bus to at least two different other cities $B$
and $C$ in $S$ (though there is not necessarily a nonstop VTRC bus from $B$ to
$A$ or from $C$ to $A$). Suppose there is a well-served subset $S$. Prove that
there is a well-served subset $T$ such that for any two cities $A, B$ in $T$,
one can travel by VTRC bus from $A$ to $B$, stopping only at cities in $T$.
